location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week in New York, NY office with regular travel to Washington DC

level_signals: 5+ years of experience building and shipping high-quality consumer fintech product experiences
Lead the vision, strategy, and execution for our Government Products portfolio
Base pay range Zone 1: $166,000–$195,000 USD, consistent with Senior IC
Role title is explicitly 'Senior Product Manager'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience building and shipping high-quality consumer fintech product experiences.
Product strategy & judgment: Strong product judgment and the ability to turn ambiguous opportunities into a clear strategy, roadmap, and execution plans.
Collaborative leadership: Excellent communication and relationship-management skills, with a track record of guiding highly cooperative teams and building alignment.
Partner & regulatory experience: Experience working with third-party partners on large-scale projects and collaborating with legal, compliance, operations, policy, or regulated-product partners.

Data-driven & mission-aligned: Experience using research, experimentation, and product analytics to guide decision-making, coupled with excitement about building products that can expand financial access at national scale.

scope.surface: Government Products portfolio, including Trump Accounts and new government partnership verticals
scope.business_model: public-private financial products, B2C consumer fintech via government partnerships
primary_metrics_inferred: Product adoption and app store rankings
User growth and financial access expansion at national scale
Quality and reliability of consumer product experience
Speed and quality of product launches
core_responsibilities: Lead product strategy: Establish and execute a clear product vision and strategy for Government Products, including scaling Trump Accounts and identifying new opportunities to expand Robinhood's impact through government partnerships.

Build & launch 0-1 products: Guide a team of engineers, designers, researchers, data scientists, and marketers to take large-scale, trusted consumer financial products from concept to national launch.

Drive internal and external collaboration: Work closely with key internal partners across legal, compliance, policy, operations, finance, and communications, as well as external partners, to ensure products are built with high quality and integrity.

Foster a culture of execution: Navigate ambiguity across product, regulatory, operational, and partnership workstreams, delivering delightful consumer experiences on time with an extremely high quality bar.
Iterate through data: Foster a culture of learning and continuous improvement through rapid experimentation, customer research, and data analysis.

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($166K–$195K base for Zone 1)
Equity and bonus programs mentioned in addition to base
Clear product surface and ownership area (Government Products portfolio, Trump Accounts)
High-visibility product with demonstrated traction (#3 app in App Store, #1 in Finance at launch)
Exciting and novel product category (public-private consumer fintech)
Comprehensive benefits package including 100% paid health insurance
red_flags: Role requires in-person attendance at least 3 days per week in New York, NY — eliminates remote candidates
Regular travel to Washington DC is expected and may be burdensome
Government partnership products carry inherent political risk (e.g., Trump Accounts branding tied to political environment)
Visa sponsorship policy not stated — unclear for international applicants
Zero-to-one scope in a regulated, politically sensitive domain adds significant ambiguity and execution risk

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, years requirement, and comp range)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — JD says 'guide a team' not 'manage direct reports')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd (Zone 1 selected as primary since role is based in New York, NY)
ambiguities: The JD lists Washington DC as a Zone 1 compensation location alongside New York, NY, but the role is described as based in New York, NY with 'regular travel to Washington DC' — DC may not be an alternative base location.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; treated as 'unclear'.

Managing people: the JD says 'guide a team of engineers, designers...' which could imply informal leadership or formal people management. Treated as non-people-manager IC role but flagged as ambiguous.
